input_row.title: Ensure all traffic is routed through the virtual network
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Networking. 6. Under Outbound traffic configuration, next to Virtual network integration, click the virtual network and subnet name. 7. Under Application routing, check the box next to Outbound internet traffic. 8. Click Apply. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to route all traffic through the virtual network: az resource update --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app- name>/slots/<deployment-slot-name> --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" -- set properties.vnetRouteAllEnabled=true Default Value: For deployment slots integrated with a virtual network, all traffic is routed through the virtual network by default. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure-vnet-integration- routing#configure-application-routing 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/webapp 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/resource CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control requires checking the configuration of virtual network integration in Azure App Service deployment slots.
- The remediation includes specific Azure CLI commands that directly manipulate the properties of the application resource.
- The Azure APIs/CLI can verify if all traffic is routed through the virtual network.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_appservice_slot_vnet_routing_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
  - The Azure CLI command to verify if traffic routing through the virtual network is enabled:
    ```bash
    az webapp deployment slot show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> --slot <deployment-slot-name> --query "properties.vnetRouteAllEnabled"
    ```

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Iterate through all Azure App Services and their associated deployment slots.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - The property `vnetRouteAllEnabled` should be evaluated. 

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** If the `vnetRouteAllEnabled` is set to `true`.
  - **Fail:** If the `vnetRouteAllEnabled` is set to `false` or is not present.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - As shown above:
    ```bash
    az webapp deployment slot show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app-name> --slot <deployment-slot-name> --query "properties.vnetRouteAllEnabled"
    ```

This program would provide an automated solution to ensure compliance with the requirement that all traffic from Azure App Service deployment slots is routed through the designated virtual network.
